timeout.description: Complete unresponsiveness — upstream holds connection open indefinitely

real_incidents.summary: Stripe API latency spike — requests hung for 30-120s before timing out
Auth0 /oauth/token endpoint experienced 60s+ hangs during EU region incident

timeout.pattern_rationale: Simulates upstream hanging connection rather than returning an error — the hardest failure for clients to detect without explicit timeouts

slow_response.description: Severe latency degradation (5-15s) — service alive but very slow

real_incidents.summary: Twilio SMS API experienced 5-30s delivery delays due to carrier gateway congestion
SendGrid mail send API latency exceeded 10s p95 for 47 minutes

slow_response.pattern_rationale: Models brownout conditions — partial degradation that passing health checks still miss

wrong_status_code.description: Incorrect HTTP status codes returned — e.g., 402 for a valid successful charge

real_incidents.summary: Stripe returned HTTP 402 for valid card charges for ~18 minutes due to internal routing bug
Stripe Checkout incorrectly returned 400 errors for successful payments during EU latency event

wrong_status_code.pattern_rationale: Applications that branch on HTTP status code instead of response body are vulnerable to this

corrupted_json.description: Malformed JSON payload — 5% of responses contain parse-breaking characters

real_incidents.summary: Twilio Lookup API returned truncated JSON responses during database failover, causing client-side parse errors
Auth0 Management API responses were intermittently truncated at 4096 bytes due to proxy buffer overflow

corrupted_json.pattern_rationale: JSON truncation or corruption during partial writes is common in database failover scenarios

silent_webhook_drop.description: Webhook delivery returns HTTP 200 but event is never processed upstream

real_incidents.summary: Stripe webhook deliveries returned 200 but were silently queued and never retried, causing payment confirmation delays of up to 4 hours
Stripe webhook endpoint returned 200 for charge.succeeded events that were dropped internally — merchants lost order fulfilment triggers

silent_webhook_drop.pattern_rationale: The most dangerous failure pattern — the caller believes the operation succeeded while the provider silently loses the event

partial_success.description: Operation succeeds but response omits critical fields (e.g., charge id missing)

real_incidents.summary: Stripe Charges API returned HTTP 200 with status=pending but no charge id, leaving merchants unable to reconcile payments

partial_success.pattern_rationale: Applications without strict response schema validation silently store incomplete records, leading to double-charge attempts on retry

rate_limit.description: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests — 20% of requests throttled

real_incidents.summary: Twilio applied aggressive rate limiting (429) to all accounts in US-East during infrastructure maintenance, affecting ~20% of SMS API calls
SendGrid rate limited shared IP pool customers at 1/5 of normal throughput for 2 hours

rate_limit.pattern_rationale: Applications without exponential backoff hammer the API and exhaust their quota — worsening the incident

auth_failure.description: JWT token expires mid-session — HTTP 401 returned for previously valid tokens

real_incidents.summary: Auth0 token signing key rotation caused all existing JWTs to be rejected as expired for 11 minutes
Auth0 US region certificate renewal caused JWKS endpoint to return expired keys, invalidating tokens for 8 minutes

auth_failure.cve_or_report: CVE-2022-23539 (JWT algorithm confusion — affects applications that do not validate alg header)
auth_failure.pattern_rationale: Applications that cache tokens indefinitely break silently when the signing key rotates

empty_response.description: HTTP 200 with completely empty response body

real_incidents.summary: SendGrid mail/send API returned HTTP 200 with empty body during Redis cluster failover — applications that assumed non-empty body crashed on JSON parse

empty_response.pattern_rationale: HTTP 200 with empty body is legal but breaks JSON-parsing clients that assume a non-empty response on success

missing_fields.description: Response body is valid JSON but critical fields are absent

real_incidents.summary: Stripe Charges API omitted the 'id' field from charge objects for ~6 minutes during a schema migration rollout, causing application-level NullPointerException crashes
Twilio Messages.json omitted the 'sid' field in responses for ~3 minutes; apps storing sid for delivery tracking silently wrote null values

missing_fields.pattern_rationale: Schema changes during rolling deployments can cause partial responses — strict field validation catches these before silent data corruption
